desc: Hurghada is Egypt’s classic Red Sea resort city, prized for warm water, coral reefs, and reliable sunshine. It suits beach holidays, beginner-to-advanced diving, and marine excursions to islands and protected snorkeling areas.
hls: Clear Red Sea water and coral reefs
Good base for desert and Bedouin excursions

todo.name: Snorkel or dive Giftun Island reefs
todo.desc: Join a boat trip to coral sites with colorful fish and high-clarity water.

todo.name: Discover dive course or fun dives
todo.desc: Use Hurghada’s many PADI centers for intro lessons, certification, or guided reef dives.

todo.name: Beach club day in Sahl Hasheesh or Makadi
todo.desc: Relax on groomed sands with calm water entries suited to long swim sessions.

todo.name: Hurghada Marina evening walk
todo.desc: Stroll cafés and waterfront promenades for sunset views and casual dining.

todo.name: Desert quad bike and Bedouin camp
todo.desc: Take a late-afternoon desert tour for dunes, tea, and stargazing outside the city lights.

todo.name: Submarine or glass-boat tour
todo.desc: A good alternative for seeing reef life without diving, especially with children.

wifi.note: Resort Wi-Fi is often uneven; eSIM/local data is useful for reliable connectivity.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick often has direct or charter-style nonstop flights to Hurghada, with extra frequencies in winter sun season.
prac.visa: Most travelers require an Egypt eVisa or visa on arrival; Red Sea package operators may provide current entry guidance.
alerts: Summer heat can exceed 40°C; schedule outdoor excursions early morning.

sweet: March to May and October to November offer warm Red Sea conditions without midsummer extremes.
